cta.label: Upload Ticket CSV -- Get the Gap Audit

hero.cta_label: Upload Ticket CSV -- Get the Gap Audit
hero.headline: Turn Repeat Support Tickets Into Approved FAQ Answers
hero.subheadline: Upload your ticket CSV and get a gap audit showing which repeat questions need FAQ coverage before your next board packet.

meta.description: Upload your support ticket CSV and get a gap audit showing which repeat questions need FAQ coverage. Review draft answers, add verified resolutions, and approve before publication.
meta.title_tag: Support Ticket FAQ Gap Audit – Turn Repeat Questions Into Answers

customer_wording_examples.text: Attribution export blocked How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?
CSV missing owner column Why is the campaign CSV export missing source owner and channel columns?
Export report for finance Can I schedule an attribution export for finance every Monday?
Dashboard export permission Why can analysts view dashboards but not export reports?
Attribution export blocked again How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?
Export filters reset Why do my saved filters disappear when I export campaign reports?

metadata.variant_angle: Social-proof-led: frame the post around observed customer or market signals without adding unsupported testimonials.

rows.persona: SaaS support leaders carrying a repeat-ticket backlog

sections.body_markdown:
In the uploaded tickets, 39 of 42 included tickets were question-shaped, with the top cluster—reporting export—appearing 11 times. Your support team already knows which questions repeat; the risk is that each repeat question costs another ticket, another response, and another chance the customer churns before they find the answer.

In the uploaded tickets, 39 of 42 included tickets were question-shaped. The top cluster—reporting export—appeared 11 times. Dashboard freshness came up 7 times. SSO setup, 6 times. Billing and plan management, 4 times.

Your support team already knows which questions repeat. The risk is that each repeat question costs another ticket, another response, and another chance the customer churns before they find the answer.

The gap audit shows you which repeat questions do not have FAQ coverage yet.

Your support team already knows which questions repeat; the risk is that each repeat question costs another ticket, another response, and another chance the customer churns before they find the answer.
metadata.primary_question: Why do the same support questions keep coming back?

sections.title: Support Teams See the Same Questions Every Week

sections.body_markdown:
Support tickets show the exact wording customers use when they hit the same problem. Six tickets can have six different phrasings for one missing FAQ answer. The gap audit preserves customer wording so you can write FAQ answers that match how your customers actually ask.

Support tickets show the exact wording customers use when they hit the same problem:

- "Attribution export blocked How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?"
- "CSV missing owner column Why is the campaign CSV export missing source owner and channel columns?"
- "Export report for finance Can I schedule an attribution export for finance every Monday?"
- "Dashboard export permission Why can analysts view dashboards but not export reports?"
- "Attribution export blocked again How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?"
- "Export filters reset Why do my saved filters disappear when I export campaign reports?"

Six tickets. Six different phrasings. One missing FAQ answer.

The gap audit preserves customer wording so you can write FAQ answers that match how your customers actually ask.

metadata.answer_summary: Support tickets show the exact wording customers use when they hit the same problem. Six tickets can have six different phrasings for one missing FAQ answer. The gap audit preserves customer wording so you can write FAQ answers that match how your customers actually ask.
metadata.primary_question: How do I know which FAQ answers to write first?

sections.title: Here's How Customers Ask the Same Question Six Different Ways

sections.body_markdown:
You upload a CSV export of your support tickets. The audit clusters repeat questions, preserves customer wording, and flags which clusters do not have FAQ coverage yet. You get the top ticket clusters ranked by repeat count, customer wording examples, draft FAQ answer shells, and a review-ready list so your support lead can approve answers before publication.

You upload a CSV export of your support tickets. The audit clusters repeat questions, preserves customer wording, and flags which clusters do not have FAQ coverage yet.

You get:

- The top ticket clusters ranked by repeat count
- Customer wording examples for each cluster
- Draft FAQ answer shells marked "Draft answer - support team should add the verified resolution before publishing"
- A review-ready list so your support lead can approve answers before publication

The audit does not publish automatically. You review the draft answers, add the verified resolution steps, and approve before anything goes live.

No chatbot setup. No full-time docs person. Just the repeat questions, the exposure, and the next action.

metadata.answer_summary: You upload a CSV export of your support tickets. The audit clusters repeat questions, preserves customer wording, and flags which clusters do not have FAQ coverage yet. You get the top ticket clusters ranked by repeat count, customer wording examples, draft FAQ answer shells, and a review-ready list so your support lead can approve answers before publication.
metadata.primary_question: What happens after I provide ticket data?

sections.title: Upload Ticket CSV, Get the Gap Audit

sections.body_markdown:
The gap audit shows you which repeat questions need FAQ coverage and drafts answer shells so you can see the structure, but it does not publish automatically. Your support lead reviews the draft answers, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves before anything goes live. You control what gets published and when.

The gap audit shows you which repeat questions need FAQ coverage. It drafts answer shells so you can see the structure. But it does not publish automatically.

Your support lead reviews the draft answers, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves before anything goes live. You control what gets published and when.

If a draft answer is incomplete or wrong, you fix it before approval. If a cluster is not worth an FAQ answer yet, you skip it.

The audit gives you the repeat questions and the exposure. You give it the verified resolution and the approval.

metadata.answer_summary: The gap audit shows you which repeat questions need FAQ coverage and drafts answer shells so you can see the structure, but it does not publish automatically. Your support lead reviews the draft answers, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves before anything goes live. You control what gets published and when.

sections.title: The Audit Does Not Publish Automatically

sections.body_markdown:
You do not need a full-time docs person to run the gap audit. You need a support lead who knows which answers are correct and a CSV export of your tickets.

The audit does the clustering, the wording extraction, and the draft answer shells. Your support lead does the verification and the approval.

If you already have a docs person, they can review the draft answers and add the verified resolution steps. If you do not, your support lead can approve answers during the same weekly review where they triage the repeat ticket backlog.

The audit does not replace your support team. It shows them which repeat questions need FAQ coverage before the next board packet.

metadata.answer_summary: You do not need a full-time docs person to run the gap audit. You need a support lead who knows which answers are correct and a CSV export of your tickets. The audit does the clustering, the wording extraction, and the draft answer shells; your support lead does the verification and the approval.

sections.title: You Do Not Need a Full-Time Docs Person

sections.body_markdown:
After you upload the ticket CSV, the audit clusters repeat questions, preserves customer wording, and flags which clusters do not have FAQ coverage yet. You get a review-ready list with the top ticket clusters ranked by repeat count, customer wording examples, and draft FAQ answer shells. Your support lead reviews the draft answers, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves before anything goes live.

After you upload the ticket CSV, the audit clusters repeat questions, preserves customer wording, and flags which clusters do not have FAQ coverage yet.

You get a review-ready list with:

- The top ticket clusters ranked by repeat count
- Customer wording examples for each cluster
- Draft FAQ answer shells marked "Draft answer - support team should add the verified resolution before publishing"

Your support lead reviews the draft answers, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves before anything goes live.

The audit does not publish automatically. You control what gets published and when.

If you want to track whether ticket volume changes after publication, you can run another gap audit after your next support cycle and compare the repeat counts.

hero.headline: Turn Repeat Tickets Into Approved FAQ Answers
hero.subheadline: Upload your support ticket CSV and get a gap audit showing which repeat questions need FAQ coverage—no guesswork, no manual sorting.

meta.description: Upload your support ticket CSV and get a gap audit showing which repeat questions need FAQ coverage. Turn repeat support tickets into approved FAQ answers.
meta.title_tag: Support Ticket FAQ Gap Audit | Cut Repeat Tickets

customer_wording_examples.text: Attribution export blocked How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?
CSV missing owner column Why is the campaign CSV export missing source owner and channel columns?
Export report for finance Can I schedule an attribution export for finance every Monday?

metadata.variant_angle: Outcome-led: lead with the business result the audience wants, then connect the supporting evidence to that result.

rows.persona: SaaS support leaders carrying a repeat-ticket backlog

sections.body_markdown:
Repeat support tickets signal missing FAQ coverage. When the same question hits your queue multiple times, you are answering the same thing over and over instead of publishing one FAQ answer that works for everyone.

Repeat support tickets signal missing FAQ coverage. In the uploaded tickets, 39 question-like tickets clustered into 11 repeat patterns. The top cluster—reporting export—appeared 11 times. Dashboard freshness came up 7 times. SSO setup, 6 times. When the same question hits your queue multiple times, you are answering the same thing over and over instead of publishing one FAQ answer that works for everyone.

The risk is not just volume. Repeat tickets mean customers could not find the answer before opening a case. That friction shows up as longer resolution times, more back-and-forth, and support capacity locked on questions you have already solved. The gap audit shows you which questions are repeating so you can prioritize the FAQ work that reduces future ticket load.

metadata.answer_summary: Repeat support tickets signal missing FAQ coverage. When the same question hits your queue multiple times, you are answering the same thing over and over instead of publishing one FAQ answer that works for everyone.
metadata.primary_question: Why do repeat support tickets matter for FAQ coverage?

sections.title: Repeat Support Tickets Signal Missing FAQ Coverage

sections.body_markdown:
The gap audit shows which repeat questions need FAQ answers. You upload your support ticket CSV, and the system clusters question-like tickets by topic, ranks them by repeat count, and surfaces the customer wording so you can prioritize FAQ work.

The gap audit shows which repeat questions need FAQ answers. You upload your support ticket CSV. The system clusters question-like tickets by topic, ranks them by repeat count, and surfaces the customer wording. You get a list of the highest-repeat question patterns with draft answer shells ready for your support team to review and approve before publishing.

The audit does not publish automatically. It does not write final procedural steps or UI paths. It gives you the repeat question evidence and a review-ready draft so your team can add the verified resolution, check accuracy, and decide when to publish. The outcome is a prioritized FAQ backlog tied to real ticket data, not a guess about what customers might ask.

metadata.answer_summary: The gap audit shows which repeat questions need FAQ answers. You upload your support ticket CSV, and the system clusters question-like tickets by topic, ranks them by repeat count, and surfaces the customer wording so you can prioritize FAQ work.
metadata.primary_question: What does the gap audit deliver?

sections.title: The Gap Audit Shows Which Repeat Questions Need FAQ Answers

sections.body_markdown:
Upload your support ticket CSV at the intake form. The system reads ticket text, filters for question-like content, clusters by topic, and ranks by repeat count. You get a gap audit report showing top repeat question clusters, customer wording examples, and draft answer shells for support review.

Upload your support ticket CSV at the intake form. The system reads ticket text, filters for question-like content, and clusters by topic. In the uploaded tickets, 42 of 44 rows were included. 39 were question-like. The system grouped them into 11 clusters and ranked by repeat count.

You get a gap audit report showing:

- Top repeat question clusters with ticket counts
- Customer wording examples from the source tickets
- Draft answer shells marked for support review

Your support team reviews each draft, adds the verified resolution steps, checks product accuracy, and approves for publication. The audit does not auto-publish or write final answers. It surfaces the repeat question evidence so you can turn the highest-risk gaps into FAQ coverage without manual ticket sorting.

metadata.answer_summary: Upload your support ticket CSV at the intake form. The system reads ticket text, filters for question-like content, clusters by topic, and ranks by repeat count. You get a gap audit report showing top repeat question clusters, customer wording examples, and draft answer shells for support review.
metadata.primary_question: How does the gap audit process work?

sections.title: How the Audit Works: Upload, Cluster, Review, Publish

sections.body_markdown:
The uploaded tickets show clear repeat patterns. Reporting export questions appeared 11 times with customer wording like:

- "Attribution export blocked How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?"
- "CSV missing owner column Why is the campaign CSV export missing source owner and channel columns?"
- "Export report for finance Can I schedule an attribution export for finance every Monday?"
- "Dashboard export permission Why can analysts view dashboards but not export reports?"

Dashboard freshness came up 7 times. SSO setup, 6 times. Billing and plan management, 4 times. Data import, 3 times. API and webhooks, 3 times. Workflow automation, permissions and seats, and integration sync each appeared twice.

These clusters show where FAQ coverage is missing. Each repeat question is a signal that customers could not find the answer before opening a ticket. The gap audit uses this evidence to prioritize which questions need FAQ answers first.
sections.title: Repeat Question Evidence from Uploaded Tickets

sections.body_markdown:
The audit does not auto-publish. It delivers draft answer shells marked for support review. Your team adds the verified resolution steps, checks product accuracy, and approves before publication. This keeps control with the people who know the product and the customer context.

The uploaded tickets contain customer questions but not verified support resolutions. The gap audit can show the repeated questions and draft review-needed FAQ answer shells, but it does not write concrete procedural steps, UI paths, menu names, permission rules, or exact fix instructions. Your support team should add the verified resolution before publishing. This review step ensures the FAQ answers are accurate and complete before customers see them.

metadata.answer_summary: The audit does not auto-publish. It delivers draft answer shells marked for support review. Your team adds the verified resolution steps, checks product accuracy, and approves before publication.

sections.title: The Audit Does Not Auto-Publish—Your Team Reviews First

sections.body_markdown:
You do not need a full-time docs person. The gap audit surfaces the repeat question evidence and draft answer shells so your existing support team can prioritize FAQ work without manual ticket sorting. The review and approval step fits into your current workflow. Your support leads or senior agents add the verified resolution, check accuracy, and publish when ready.

The audit reduces the manual work of finding which questions repeat and how often. It does not replace your team's product knowledge or judgment. It gives you the data so you can focus review time on the highest-repeat gaps instead of sorting through every ticket by hand.

metadata.answer_summary: You do not need a full-time docs person. The gap audit surfaces the repeat question evidence and draft answer shells so your existing support team can prioritize FAQ work without manual ticket sorting.
metadata.primary_question: Do we need a full-time docs person?

sections.title: No Full-Time Docs Person Required

sections.body_markdown:
After you provide ticket data, the system clusters question-like tickets by topic, ranks them by repeat count, and generates a gap audit report showing top repeat question clusters with customer wording examples and draft answer shells for support review.

After you provide ticket data, the system clusters question-like tickets by topic, ranks them by repeat count, and generates a gap audit report. You get the top repeat question clusters with customer wording examples and draft answer shells marked for support review. The report shows which questions are repeating so you can prioritize FAQ coverage.

Your support team reviews each draft, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves for publication. The audit does not auto-publish or write final answers. It surfaces the repeat question evidence so you can turn the highest-risk gaps into FAQ coverage without manual ticket sorting. The whole process—from upload to gap audit delivery—takes a few minutes. Review and approval timing depends on your team's workflow.

metadata.answer_summary: After you provide ticket data, the system clusters question-like tickets by topic, ranks them by repeat count, and generates a gap audit report showing top repeat question clusters with customer wording examples and draft answer shells for support review.
metadata.primary_question: What happens after I provide ticket data?

sections.title: What Happens After I Provide Ticket Data?

sections.body_markdown:
Upload your support ticket CSV and get the gap audit showing which repeat questions need FAQ coverage. The audit surfaces the highest-repeat question patterns with customer wording and draft answer shells so your support team can prioritize FAQ work without manual ticket sorting.

The process takes a few minutes. You get a prioritized FAQ backlog tied to real ticket data. Your team reviews, adds verified resolutions, and publishes when ready. No auto-publish. No guesswork. Just the repeat question evidence you need to turn support tickets into FAQ answers.

The process takes a few minutes. You get a prioritized FAQ backlog tied to real ticket data. Your team reviews, adds verified resolutions, and publishes when ready. No auto-publish. No guesswork. Just the repeat question evidence you need to turn support tickets into FAQ answers.

@graph.name: Support Ticket FAQ Gap Audit | Cut Repeat Tickets

potentialAction.name: Upload Ticket CSV -- Get the Gap Audit

acceptedAnswer.text: Repeat support tickets signal missing FAQ coverage. When the same question hits your queue multiple times, you are answering the same thing over and over instead of publishing one FAQ answer that works for everyone.

mainEntity.name: Why do repeat support tickets matter for FAQ coverage?

acceptedAnswer.text: The gap audit shows which repeat questions need FAQ answers. You upload your support ticket CSV, and the system clusters question-like tickets by topic, ranks them by repeat count, and surfaces the customer wording so you can prioritize FAQ work.

mainEntity.name: What does the gap audit deliver?

acceptedAnswer.text: Upload your support ticket CSV at the intake form. The system reads ticket text, filters for question-like content, clusters by topic, and ranks by repeat count. You get a gap audit report showing top repeat question clusters, customer wording examples, and draft answer shells for support review.

mainEntity.name: How does the gap audit process work?

acceptedAnswer.text: The audit does not auto-publish. It delivers draft answer shells marked for support review. Your team adds the verified resolution steps, checks product accuracy, and approves before publication.

You do not need a full-time docs person. The gap audit surfaces the repeat question evidence and draft answer shells so your existing support team can prioritize FAQ work without manual ticket sorting.

mainEntity.name: Do we need a full-time docs person?

acceptedAnswer.text: After you provide ticket data, the system clusters question-like tickets by topic, ranks them by repeat count, and generates a gap audit report showing top repeat question clusters with customer wording examples and draft answer shells for support review.

customer_wording_examples.text: Attribution export blocked How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?
CSV missing owner column Why is the campaign CSV export missing source owner and channel columns?
Export report for finance Can I schedule an attribution export for finance every Monday?
Dashboard export permission Why can analysts view dashboards but not export reports?
Attribution export blocked again How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?
Export filters reset Why do my saved filters disappear when I export campaign reports?

metadata.variant_angle: Pain-led: open with the customer's current friction, then show how the evidence supports the recommended next step.

rows.persona: SaaS support leaders carrying a repeat-ticket backlog

sections.body_markdown:
Your support team closes tickets, then sees the same questions again next week. In the uploaded tickets, 39 out of 42 included tickets were question-shaped, with repeat patterns in reporting export, dashboard freshness, and SSO setup. Each repeat question is a gap in your help center that costs your team time and your customers friction.

Your support team closes tickets, then sees the same questions again next week. In the uploaded tickets, 39 out of 42 included tickets were question-shaped. The top repeat patterns were reporting export (11 tickets), dashboard freshness (7 tickets), and SSO setup (6 tickets). Each repeat question is a gap in your help center that costs your team time and your customers friction.

When customers ask "How do I export attribution reports before the board packet is due?" or "Why is the campaign CSV export missing source owner and channel columns?" multiple times, the problem is not the customer. The problem is the missing answer.

You already carry the backlog. The question is whether you turn those repeat tickets into review-ready FAQ answers or keep answering them one ticket at a time.

metadata.answer_summary: Your support team closes tickets, then sees the same questions again next week. In the uploaded tickets, 39 out of 42 included tickets were question-shaped, with repeat patterns in reporting export, dashboard freshness, and SSO setup. Each repeat question is a gap in your help center that costs your team time and your customers friction.
metadata.primary_question: Why do we keep answering the same support questions?

sections.title: The repeat-ticket pattern your support team already knows

sections.body_markdown:
The uploaded ticket data included 44 rows, with 42 tickets analyzed and 39 question-like tickets. The top repeat clusters were reporting export (11 tickets), dashboard freshness (7 tickets), and SSO setup (6 tickets). Each cluster is a candidate FAQ topic your help center does not answer yet.

The uploaded ticket data included 44 rows. 42 tickets were included in the analysis. 2 tickets were skipped because they were not question-shaped or did not contain enough context. 39 tickets were question-like.

The top ticket clusters were:

- **Reporting export** (11 tickets): "Attribution export blocked", "CSV missing owner column", "Export report for finance", "Dashboard export permission", "Attribution export blocked again", "Export filters reset"
- **Dashboard freshness** (7 tickets)
- **SSO setup** (6 tickets)
- **Billing and plan management** (4 tickets)
- **Data import** (3 tickets)
- **API and webhooks** (3 tickets)
- **Workflow automation** (2 tickets)
- **Permissions and seats** (2 tickets)
- **Integration sync** (2 tickets)

Each cluster is a candidate FAQ topic. The customer wording shows the friction: "Why can analysts view dashboards but not export reports?" and "Why do my saved filters disappear when I export campaign reports?" are not edge cases. They are repeat questions your help center does not answer yet.

metadata.answer_summary: The uploaded ticket data included 44 rows, with 42 tickets analyzed and 39 question-like tickets. The top repeat clusters were reporting export (11 tickets), dashboard freshness (7 tickets), and SSO setup (6 tickets). Each cluster is a candidate FAQ topic your help center does not answer yet.
metadata.primary_question: What does the ticket data actually show?

sections.title: What the ticket data shows

sections.body_markdown:
The gap audit turns your ticket CSV into a ranked list of repeat questions and draft FAQ answer shells. You see repeat question clusters ranked by ticket count, customer wording examples from the tickets, and draft answer shells marked as review-needed. Your support team adds the verified resolution before publishing.

The Support Ticket FAQ Gap Audit turns your ticket CSV into a ranked list of repeat questions and draft FAQ answer shells. You see:

1. **Repeat question clusters** ranked by ticket count, so you know which gaps cost the most support time.
2. **Customer wording examples** pulled from the tickets, so your FAQ answers match the language customers actually use.
3. **Draft answer shells** for each cluster, marked as review-needed. The draft shows the question and the customer context. Your support team adds the verified resolution before publishing.

The audit does not publish automatically. It does not write final procedural steps or UI paths. It shows you the repeat questions and gives you the draft structure. Your team reviews, adds the verified answer, and approves publication.

You do not need a full-time docs person to run the audit. You need a ticket CSV and 20 minutes to review the output.

metadata.answer_summary: The gap audit turns your ticket CSV into a ranked list of repeat questions and draft FAQ answer shells. You see repeat question clusters ranked by ticket count, customer wording examples from the tickets, and draft answer shells marked as review-needed. Your support team adds the verified resolution before publishing.
metadata.primary_question: What does the gap audit give me?

sections.title: What you get from the gap audit

sections.body_markdown:
Upload your ticket CSV with ticket title, body, and resolution text. The audit clusters question-like tickets by topic and ranks them by repeat count. You get the gap report with repeat question clusters, customer wording examples, and draft answer shells. Your support team reviews the draft answers, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves publication.

1. **Upload your ticket CSV.** Include ticket title, body, and any resolution or answer text your support team wrote. The audit needs enough context to identify repeat questions.
2. **The audit clusters question-like tickets** by topic and ranks them by repeat count.
3. **You get the gap report** with repeat question clusters, customer wording examples, and draft answer shells.
4. **Your support team reviews the draft answers**, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves publication.

The audit does not access your live support system. It does not publish answers without review. It does not write final procedural guidance. It shows you the repeat questions and the draft structure. You decide what gets published and when.

metadata.answer_summary: Upload your ticket CSV with ticket title, body, and resolution text. The audit clusters question-like tickets by topic and ranks them by repeat count. You get the gap report with repeat question clusters, customer wording examples, and draft answer shells. Your support team reviews the draft answers, adds the verified resolution steps, and approves publication.
metadata.primary_question: How does the gap audit work?

sections.title: How the audit works

sections.body_markdown:
No. The audit produces draft answer shells marked as review-needed. Your support team reviews each draft, adds the verified resolution, and approves publication. The audit does not publish automatically. You control what gets published and when.

No. The audit produces draft answer shells marked as review-needed. Your support team reviews each draft, adds the verified resolution, and approves publication. The audit does not publish automatically. It does not write final procedural steps or UI paths. It shows you the repeat questions and the draft structure. You control what gets published and when.

metadata.answer_summary: No. The audit produces draft answer shells marked as review-needed. Your support team reviews each draft, adds the verified resolution, and approves publication. The audit does not publish automatically. You control what gets published and when.

sections.title: Does the audit publish FAQ answers automatically?

sections.body_markdown:
No. The audit takes a ticket CSV and 20 minutes to review the output. Your support team already knows the answers. The audit shows you which questions repeat most often and gives you the draft structure. You do not need a dedicated docs role to run the audit or publish the answers.

No. The audit takes a ticket CSV and 20 minutes to review the output. Your support team already knows the answers. The audit shows you which questions repeat most often and gives you the draft structure. Your team adds the verified resolution and approves publication. You do not need a dedicated docs role to run the audit or publish the answers.

metadata.answer_summary: No. The audit takes a ticket CSV and 20 minutes to review the output. Your support team already knows the answers. The audit shows you which questions repeat most often and gives you the draft structure. You do not need a dedicated docs role to run the audit or publish the answers.
metadata.primary_question: Do we need a full-time docs person?

sections.title: Do we need a full-time docs person to use this?

sections.body_markdown:
Upload your ticket CSV and get the gap audit. You will see the repeat question clusters, the customer wording, and the draft answer shells. Your support team reviews the drafts, adds the verified resolutions, and approves publication.

The audit does not promise future ticket volume will drop. It does not claim customers will stop opening tickets. It shows you the repeat questions your help center does not answer yet and gives you the draft structure to fix the gap.

If you want to track whether ticket volume changes after publication, you can run the audit again with a later ticket window and compare the repeat question counts.
